pi_items.source: task.json description, setup_task.sh, export_result.sh (line 42 queries idnumber='DLF001'), verifier.py (line 51 references DLF001)
task.json description states framework name should be 'Digital Literacy Framework', verifier.py (line 69) checks if 'Digital Literacy' in name, README.md confirms 'Digital Literacy Framework'

task.json description lists three competencies with these exact ID numbers, export_result.sh (lines 65, 70, 75) checks for each ID individually, export_result.sh (line 96) validates these IDs in course links
task.json description mentions CS101, setup_task.sh (line 46) creates course with shortname='CS101', export_result.sh (line 84) queries WHERE shortname='CS101'

task.json description states course full name as 'Introduction to Computer Science', setup_task.sh (line 45) creates course with fullname='Introduction to Computer Science', README.md (line 31) confirms this fullname

privileged_info_summary: This task involves creating a Moodle competency framework with ID number DLF001 and name containing 'Digital Literacy'. The framework must contain three competencies with ID numbers DL-IL, DL-DC, and DL-DA (representing Information Literacy, Digital Communication, and Data Analysis Basics respectively). These competencies are then linked to the course CS101 (Introduction to Computer Science). Moodle 4.5 is the underlying platform, which natively supports competency frameworks through database tables mdl_competency_framework, mdl_competency, and mdl_competency_coursecomp. All metadata values are verified as accurate and consistent across task definition, setup scripts, export scripts, and verification logic.
